title: Scrambling in Joshua Tree (The Sequel): Even More Scrambling
summary:
After leaving the beach, we braved the rushhour LA traffic and headed back to Joshua Tree. Our campsite at the Jumbo Rocks Campground was nestled into some big boulders, and we enjoyed our morning coffee while reading on a big sunny rock. Our first day in the park, Abby graciously watched Maya while Helen and I climbed a 5.6 multipitch called Right On. It was Helen's first multipitch and I was giddy to get to share one of my activities with her. The climb went relatively smoothly, except for the part where I got us slightly off route and dropped a piece of gear down a crack (which I was luckily able to retrieve). Other than that, it was an awesome outing, and I told Helen at least 5 times that 'I'm having so much fun!!!' We spent one more day climbing at a crag called Conan's Corridor where we did a few fun crack climbs, but ended up bailing early since it was windy, shady and cold.

In the evenings, we cooked some group meals with Abby, including fire roasted brats and a yummy chicken curry. After dinner, we busted out Abby's projector and watched some movies projected on a sheet inside our van. We watched The Woman in Cabin 10, an entertaining thriller, and Sorry, Baby, which we all loved and highly recommend. On our last day, Helen and I biked from our campsite to a trailhead and hiked to an old abandoned mine. We had a great time in Joshua Tree and I'm sure we'll be back sometime soon!

photos.caption: A selfie after climbing an awesome 4-pitch 5.6 called 'Right On'. The climb followed the right of the two parallel cracks on the rock in the background.
Colin, Maya and Wynn relaxing in bed.

Colin posing with some old mining machinery in Joshua Tree.
Biking to a trailhead in Joshua Tree.
A neat rock feature on the Skull Rock Trail.
Colin starting up a crack climb.
